name: Asparagus Oyster
recipeIngredient: Fresh asparagus - 6-7 spears per person (not too thin, not too old)
Fresh or frozen oysters - 3 per person
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the bottom inch of the asparagus spears and peel the lower part if necessary.
2. Poach the asparagus gently in about an inch of boiling water for 7 minutes, or use a European-style tall asparagus boiler.
3. Cut the oysters into thin strips.
4. Reserve the juice from the oyster shells (about 2 tablespoons from 6 oysters).
5. Mix the reserved juice with the dry white wine and reduce by about two-thirds over low heat until reduced to about 1 and a half tablespoons.
6. Add this liquor and about 4 tablespoons of poaching water slowly to the egg yolks, whisking gently on very low heat until light and frothy.
7. Add seasoning (salt, pepper, cayenne if desired).
8. Stir in 20 grams of cold diced butter until smooth.
9. Fold in the oyster strips.
10. Serve the freshly cooked asparagus spears with the oyster sauce poured over them.
